title: User Tracking and Authentication Events
interaction_flow: Application → DDTrace\active_span(): SpanData|null — retrieve the current active span
Application → DDTrace\root_span(): RootSpanData|null — retrieve the root span of the active stack
Application → DDTrace\set_user(userId, metadata[], propagate): void — record user identity on root span (usr.id + usr.<TAG_NAME> in meta section)
DDTrace\set_user → RootSpanData: writes usr.id + optional metadata tags to span.meta[]
DDTrace\set_user → [if propagate=true]: propagates user info as distributed trace headers
Application → DDTrace\add_distributed_tag(key, value): void — add propagated tag to distributed traces AND local root span
DDTrace\add_distributed_tag → RootSpanData: writes tag to span.meta[] and propagates downstream
Application → datadog\appsec\v2\track_user_login_success(login, user, metadata[]): void — record successful login event
datadog\appsec\v2\track_user_login_success → RootSpanData: appends user metadata tags to root span
Application → datadog\appsec\v2\track_user_login_failure(login, exists, metadata[]): void — record failed login event
datadog\appsec\v2\track_user_login_failure → RootSpanData: appends failure metadata tags to root span

SpanData.name: string|null — span name
SpanData.meta: string[] — key-value metadata (usr.* tags written here)

RootSpanData.origin: string — origin site of the trace, propagated in distributed tracing

key_relationships: DDTrace\add_distributed_tag writes to RootSpanData AND propagates to downstream services
datadog\appsec\v2 functions write metadata to root span

parameter_notes.set_user.$userId: usr.id tag value — unique identifier for the user
parameter_notes.set_user.$metadata: arbitrary usr.<TAG_NAME> key-value pairs applied to root span meta
parameter_notes.set_user.$propagate: true = user info forwarded as distributed trace headers; null = default behavior
parameter_notes.track_user_login_success.$user: string = user id; array must contain 'id' key matching set_user format
parameter_notes.track_user_login_failure.$exists: boolean indicating if user account exists in the system

namespaces.DDTrace: Core tracing functions — span management, user identity, tag propagation
namespaces.datadog\\appsec\\v2: AppSec (Application Security) functions — login event tracking

important_notes: set_user() targets the root span directly. active_span() returns the innermost span on the stack. root_span() returns the outermost span of the active stack. add_distributed_tag() both tags the local root span AND propagates downstream via distributed tracing headers. track_user_login_success/failure are AppSec v2 API functions, separate from core DDTrace namespace. The user id format between set_user and track_user_login_success must be consistent.
